_spawn_isolation_note: Loop body inline in main (Opus); Step-3 reviewer + HALT challenger spawned as independent subagents.
verdict_explanation: With the search orchestration tested last loop, this loop extends the fetch seam to SoundboardSnag and tests its guard and abort logic offline, fully resolving F-008. The codebase is now a thin, fully-tested decomposition: every pure helper and both network-orchestration paths have direct tests, ownership is single-writer throughout, and concurrency is trivially safe. The remaining deductions are honest design ceilings (domain modeling of a 2-tuple, a single-file synchronous CLI) plus one cosmetic redundant filter, not structural hazards.

architecture_quality.proof: fetch seam added to search_boards with two real adapters (commit 74545e9); the module graph is now pure-helpers + orchestration with injectable I/O. Residual: SoundboardSnag pipeline lacked a seam at loop start (fixed this loop)

state_management.proof: single-threaded; one writer per concern; SoundboardSnag attrs immutable post-__init__

domain_modeling.proof: BoardResult + ParsedBoard name the records; sounds_info is a deliberate plain (id,title) 2-tuple — typing it further would be ceremony the rubric warns against (state/domain guardrail)

data_flow.proof: network effect is now an explicit injected fetch dependency rather than ambient urlopen (commit 74545e9). Residual: main re-filters results (soundboard-snag.py:1601) duplicating search_boards' guarantee (F-007)

framework_idioms.proof: idiomatic stdlib; defensive sanitization; HTTPS; no secrets; broad except blocks log their errors

concurrency.proof: no async/threading — synchronous CLI, no shared-mutable hazard
concurrency.residual_blocking_10: time.sleep pacing is intentional server etiquette, not a determinism hazard
concurrency.residual_rationale_or_backlog_ref: Tool is deliberately synchronous; no concurrency model to make safer. Permanent design carve-out.

simplicity.proof: search_boards is an orchestration core; all pure logic in small tested helpers. Residual: one redundant re-filter in main (F-007)

test_strategy.proof: SearchBoardsOrchestrationTests added (commit 74545e9) — pagination, dedup, view-sort, play-only exclusion, min-views, early-stop tested offline; 50 tests. Residual at loop start: SoundboardSnag pipeline untested (fixed this loop)

credibility.proof: every Module is test-backed; the fetch seam follows the two-adapter rule; no fake-clean anywhere in the refactor history

strengths: Both network-orchestration surfaces (search_boards and SoundboardSnag.snag) are now testable offline through a single injected-function seam — no class ceremony
The SoundboardSnag guard logic (play-only detection, no-audio, the 2-consecutive-failure abort) is directly tested
Eight consecutive behavior-preserving, independently-reviewed refactors with a growing test suite — an honest, auditable trail

findings.title: Network orchestration and the download pipeline are untestable (no injectable fetch seam)
findings.why_it_matters: The behavior-rich orchestration and the SoundboardSnag guard/abort logic could only be exercised against the live site.
findings.what_is_wrong: search_boards (last loop) and SoundboardSnag (this loop) called urlopen directly with no injection point for the page fetch.
findings.leverage_impact: Without a seam, the guard/abort logic could not be verified offline.
findings.locality_impact: A regression in play-only detection or the failure-abort would only surface against the live site.
findings.why_weakens_submission: Was the remaining test-strategy/architecture ceiling. A fetch seam (real _http_get + in-memory fake) satisfies the two-adapter rule on both surfaces and unlocks deterministic tests.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Add a fetcher parameter to SoundboardSnag.__init__ defaulting to _http_get; route _fetch_page through it; test play-only RuntimeError, no-audio RuntimeError, the 2-consecutive-failure abort (mocking _snag_sound), and HTTP-error wrapping. The binary download and the HEAD date-probe remain real leaf I/O.

findings.title: main re-filters search results that are already downloadable-only
findings.why_it_matters: A redundant filter implies search_boards might return play-only boards, which it does not; the duplication is mildly misleading.
findings.what_is_wrong: main computes downloadable_boards = [r for r in results if r.has_downloads], but search_boards already returns only has_downloads boards.
evidence: soundboard-snag.py: search_boards returns results filtered by has_downloads
findings.leverage_impact: None — the filter is a no-op.
findings.locality_impact: Minor: a reader may wonder whether search_boards can return play-only boards.
findings.why_weakens_submission: Redundant defensive code is a small data-flow/simplicity deduction; the last remaining backlog item.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Replace the re-filter with downloadable_boards = results (or iterate results directly), with a comment that search_boards already guarantees has_downloads.

simplification_check.structurally_necessary: Extending the fetch seam to SoundboardSnag passes the two-adapter rule (real _http_get + in-memory fake used by the new tests) and is the only way to verify the guard/abort logic offline. _fetch_page collapses to self.fetcher(url) wrapped in the same RuntimeError mapping.
simplification_check.helpful_simplification: Dropped the effectively-unreachable getcode()!=200 branch (urlopen raises HTTPError for non-2xx; 3xx are followed), so the error mapping is unchanged for all reachable cases.
simplification_check.should_not_be_done: Do not route the binary download (_snag_sound) or the HEAD date-probe through the text fetcher (they are byte/headers I/O, not text); do not add a client class hierarchy.
simplification_check.tests_after_fix: test_soundboard_snag.py gains SnagPipelineTests (play-only RuntimeError, no-audio RuntimeError, 2-consecutive-failure abort via mocked _snag_sound, URLError->RuntimeError wrapping).

backlog.title: Inject a fetcher into SoundboardSnag and test the guard/abort logic (F-008 residual)
backlog.why_it_matters: Completes the testability seam; the download pipeline's guard logic gets a regression guard.

backlog.title: Remove the redundant downloadable re-filter in main (F-007)
backlog.why_it_matters: Last backlog item; subtractive; clarifies search_boards already guarantees downloadable-only.

builder_notes.pattern: Extend an established seam to the sibling surface
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: One network surface was made injectable; a second one (a class) still calls the network directly.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Reuse the same default fetcher and pass it as a constructor parameter; route the page fetch through self.fetcher.

builder_notes.pattern: Drop an unreachable defensive branch when its observable behavior is unchanged
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: A status-code check sits after a call that already raises on the same condition.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Confirm the branch is unreachable (urlopen raises HTTPError for non-2xx; redirects are followed), then remove it and update the docstring.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: the getcode()!=200 RuntimeError after urlopen never fired for this site.

builder_notes.pattern: Mock the leaf method to test the loop around it
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: The logic under test (a retry/abort loop) calls a method that does real I/O you do not want to fake at the byte level.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: mock.patch.object the leaf method to return canned outcomes; assert the loop's control flow (call count, abort).
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: patch _snag_sound to return (False, 'boom'); assert it is called exactly twice before the abort.

narrative: Win territory — a strong contender now. This loop completes the testability story: SoundboardSnag gets the same injected-fetcher seam, and its play-only, no-audio, and consecutive-failure-abort branches are directly tested. Every pure helper and both network-orchestration surfaces have regression guards; ownership is single-writer; concurrency is trivially safe; the refactor never once reached for ceremony. The remaining sub-9.5 dimensions are honest design ceilings (a deliberately plain 2-tuple, a single-file synchronous CLI) plus one cosmetic redundant filter — not structural hazards. The last loop should clear F-007 and the run should halt at the cap with a clear residual statement.

loop_result.what_changed: Added a fetcher parameter to SoundboardSnag.__init__ (default _http_get) and routed _fetch_page through self.fetcher, dropping the effectively-unreachable getcode()!=200 branch (error mapping unchanged). Updated the _fetch_page docstring to match. Added SnagPipelineTests (4 tests): play-only board raises 'downloads disabled', empty page raises 'No audio files found', five downloadable sounds with a mocked failing _snag_sound abort after exactly 2 consecutive failures, and a URLError from the fetcher is wrapped as RuntimeError 'Network error'.
loop_result.evidence_change_is_honest: python3 -m py_compile passes; python3 -m unittest test_soundboard_snag runs 54 tests, all OK (50 prior + 4 new); --help exits 0; on a real HTTP error the path is identical (self.fetcher==_http_get -> urlopen raises HTTPError -> same RuntimeError 'HTTP Error {code}: {reason}'); the only removed branch (getcode()!=200) was unreachable because urlopen raises HTTPError for non-2xx and follows 3xx; grep confirms _fetch_page no longer calls urlopen (only _http_get, the HEAD date-probe, and the binary download retain urlopen).

implementation_review.reason: All three checks pass: the fetch seam is correctly in place on SoundboardSnag, the dropped getcode()!=200 branch was genuinely unreachable (urlopen raises HTTPError for non-2xx and follows 3xx), two real adapters exist (_http_get + behavior-faithful fake), the docstring is updated, and all four SnagPipelineTests assert real behavior.
